title: PostgreSQL 18 Schema, Constraints, Keys, and Query-Driven Indexes · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A disability-led research network incrementally builds constrained TypeScript feed-service relational model from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and browser fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, HTTP-client, Express, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, async-cleanup, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of constrained TypeScript feed-service relational model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through p…
objectives: Model tenants, principals, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, versions, people, categories, fetches, leases, and reader state with explicit ownership.
Use stable internal identifiers plus tenant-scoped external identities and preserve URL moves, aliases, and provenance.
Encode required fields, lengths, checks, states, unique scopes, foreign keys, cascades, and temporal rules in PostgreSQL.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Relational Domain
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 7083 during a clean emitted-package and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile relational entities, stable keys, constraints, tenant scope, and query-driven indexes; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant work must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, entity, date, identity, or runtime-validation case and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Relational Domain.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model tenants, principals, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, versions, people, categories, fetches, leases, and reader state with explicit ownership. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Relational Domain, keep Feed Ts Relational Domain connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
